Arcane: An ARC of ANcient secrets, understood by few
Ascetic: An ASCETIC is 'a-set-ic' on self-denial, no comforts
Austere: AUSTERE sounds stern — strict, bare, no luxury
Banal: BANAL is 'ho-hum' — a BANANA joke told a thousand times
Beguile: BEGUILE hides GUILE — charm you while deceiving
Bolster: A BOLSTER pillow props you up — support, strengthen
Boorish: A BOOR is rough — 'boar-ish', crude and bad-mannered
Callous: A CALLUS is hard skin — CALLOUS is a hardened, unfeeling heart
Caustic: CAUSTIC acid burns — words that scorch and corrode
Clandestine: CLANDESTINE — a secret CLAN meeting, hidden and illicit
Coerce: COERCE forces you — 'co-ERASE' your choice by threat
Conflagration: CONFLAGRATION — a huge FLAGRant fire, all-consuming
Contemptuous: CONTEMPTUOUS looks down — full of CONTEMPT and scorn
Corroborate: CORROBORATE backs you up — a co-ROBOT confirms your story
Credulous: CREDULOUS believes anything — gives CREDit too easily
Culpable: CULPABLE is guilty — the CULPrit deserves blame
Daunt: DAUNT intimidates — a 'daunting' task makes you shrink
Deleterious: DELETERIOUS deletes your health — harmful, damaging
Delineate: DELINEATE draws the LINE — describe precisely
Denigrate: DENIGRATE blackens a name — 'de-NIGR', to disparage
Derivative: DERIVATIVE is deRIVED from another — not original
Desiccate: DESICCATE dries out — think 'dry-SICK-ate', all moisture gone
Diatribe: A DIATRIBE is a bitter TRIBAl rant, a verbal attack
Diffident: DIFFIDENT lacks confidence — shy, not confident
Discern: DISCERN spots the difference — 'dis-CERN', perceive clearly
Dispassionate: DISPASSIONATE has no PASSION — cool, impartial
Disseminate: DISSEMINATE spreads SEEDs of information widely
Dogmatic: DOGMATIC clings to DOGMA — rigidly certain, no doubt
Dormant: DORMANT sleeps — a DORMitory is for sleeping, inactive
Perennial: PERENNIAL returns every year — a lasting, enduring plant
Perspicacious: PERSPICACIOUS sees through — sharp, perceptive insight
Pertinent: PERTINENT perTAINS to the point — relevant, applicable
Precipitate: PRECIPITATE happens suddenly — like rain PRECIPITation, abrupt
Preclude: PRECLUDE shuts the door before — prevents, makes impossible
Predilection: PREDILECTION is a leaning toward — a special liking, bias
Preeminent: PREEMINENT stands above all — 'pre-EMINENT', most distinguished
Prevail: PREVAIL wins out — proves superior, triumphs
Prudent: PRUDENT is careful — a PRUDE plays it safe, thinks ahead
Punctilious: PUNCTILIOUS is fussy about every POINT — precise on detail
Relegate: RELEGATE demotes — sent to a LEGGY lower rank
Remiss: REMISS MISSed the duty — careless, negligent
Replete: REPLETE is full — completely stocked and supplied
Reprieve: REPRIEVE gives relief — punishment postponed, a breather
Resolute: RESOLUTE is firm — full of RESOLVE, unwavering
Reticent: RETICENT holds back — reserved, doesn't reveal feelings
Salient: SALIENT jumps out — the most prominent, noticeable point
Sanction: SANCTION is two-faced — official approval, OR a penalty
Scrutinize: SCRUTINIZE inspects closely — SCREW your eyes in, examine
Servile: SERVILE over-serves — a SERVant grovelling to please
Skeptical: SKEPTICAL doubts — not easily convinced, questions it
Solemn: SOLEMN is grave — a SOLE, serious dignity
Solvent: SOLVENT can pay debts — or dissolves other substances
Steadfast: STEADFAST holds STEADY and FAST — firm, unwavering
Stringent: STRINGENT is tight — strict rules pulled STRING-tight
Subjugate: SUBJUGATE forces under a YOKE — conquer and control
Sublime: SUBLIME is lofty grandeur — inspires awe and admiration
Substantiate: SUBSTANTIATE gives SUBSTANCE — evidence to prove it
Subtle: SUBTLE is delicate — the B is silent, hard to detect
Succinct: SUCCINCT is tight — 'suck it in', brief and concise
Superficial: SUPERFICIAL stays on the surface — like a FACIAL, skin-deep
Supplant: SUPPLANT replaces — a new PLANT takes the old one's place
Suppress: SUPPRESS pushes down — PRESS it under, keep it hidden
Surmise: SURMISE is a guess — 'sir, my-eyes' suppose without proof
Tantamount: TANTAMOUNT equals — 'amounts to' the same thing
Temperate: TEMPERATE keeps its TEMPER — moderate, restrained
Temporal: TEMPORAL is of time and the world — 'tempo', earthly not spiritual
Tenable: TENABLE can be held — a position you can TEN-d and defend
Tentative: TENTATIVE is unsure — pitched like a TENT, provisional
Timorous: TIMOROUS is timid — full of TIMor, nervous and fearful
Tirade: A TIRADE tires you — a long, angry rant
Tractable: TRACTABLE is manageable — easy to TRACT, to lead
Tranquil: TRANQUIL is calm — a peaceful, TRANQUILizer stillness
Trite: TRITE is stale — worn out, overused, unoriginal
Truncate: TRUNCATE chops off — cut the TRUNK short
Turbulent: TURBULENT is stormy — TURBO chaos, unstable
Tyranny: TYRANNY oppresses — a cruel TYRANT's iron rule
Unassuming: UNASSUMING is modest — makes no big assumptions about itself
Unconscionable: UNCONSCIONABLE has no CONSCIENCE — shockingly unfair
Undermine: UNDERMINE digs UNDER — erodes the base, weakens
Undulate: UNDULATE waves — moves like UNDUlating water
Unfettered: UNFETTERED is free — no FETTERs, no chains restrict it
Unilateral: UNILATERAL is one-sided — 'UNI', one party acts alone
Unimpeachable: UNIMPEACHABLE can't be doubted — you can't IMPEACH it
Unprecedented: UNPRECEDENTED never happened before — no PRECEDENT exists
Unscrupulous: UNSCRUPULOUS has no SCRUPLES — no morals, unfair
Urbane: URBANE is polished — smooth, URBAN city sophistication
Usurp: USURP grabs power — 'you-SURP' the throne by force
Utilitarian: UTILITARIAN is useful — built for UTILITY, not beauty
Utopian: UTOPIAN dreams of perfect — an ideal UTOPIA, unreal
Vehement: VEHEMENT is intense — forceful passion, driven like a VEHicle
Venerable: VENERABLE is revered — respected for age, like a VETERAN
Venerate: VENERATE worships — to reVERE with great respect
Verbose: VERBOSE is wordy — too many VERBs and words
Viable: VIABLE can live — has 'VITA', life, workable and feasible
Vigilant: VIGILANT watches — keeps VIGIL, alert to danger
Vindicate: VINDICATE clears you — proves you were right all along
Vindictive: VINDICTIVE seeks revenge — a vengeful 'vin-DICT', spiteful
Virulent: VIRULENT is toxic — a deadly VIRUS, severe and hostile
Visceral: VISCERAL is gut-deep — felt in the VISCERA, not the mind
Vitriolic: VITRIOLIC burns — acid VITRIOL words, bitter malice
Volatile: VOLATILE flips fast — like a VOLcano, unstable, explosive
Volition: VOLITION is free will — your own conscious choice
Voracious: VORACIOUS devours — a 'vora' hunger, insatiable eater
Vulnerable: VULNERABLE can be wounded — open to a 'vulner', harm
Wane: WANE shrinks — the moon WANEs, declines and fades
Welter: A WELTER is chaos — a jumbled, confused mass
Whimsical: WHIMSICAL follows a WHIM — playful, fanciful, quaint
Winsome: WINSOME WINS you over — charming, appealing
Wistful: WISTFUL is longing — a wishful, regretful yearning
Withhold: WITHHOLD keeps back — HOLD it and refuse to give
Wizened: WIZENED is shrivelled — a wrinkled old WIZard's face
Wrath: WRATH is fury — extreme rage, divine vengeance
Wretched: WRETCHED is miserable — a poor, unhappy WRETCH
Writhe: WRITHE twists — squirm and WRIThe in pain
Yearn: YEARN aches for — a deep 'year-long' longing
Yielding: YIELDING gives way — bends under pressure, submissive
Zenith: ZENITH is the peak — the highest point, straight overhead
